content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Found 2 medium fix-now regressions in the duplicate-active-bead hardening: the create-time guard ignores pending lineage resets, and it still permits legacy runless orphan rows to slip through.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: Create-time duplicate detection reads `task-runs.ndjson` even when the milestone snapshot still has `pending_lineage_reset`, so rematerialized plans can be blocked by stale same-bead lineage that `record_bead_start` would clear before any start.
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
file_refs: ["src/cli/project.rs:351", "src/cli/project.rs:440", "src/cli/project.rs:583-593", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:411-431", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2988-3000"]
remediation_hint: Before `reject_duplicate_active_bead_creation` trusts lineage, honor `pending_lineage_reset` the same way `record_bead_start` does, or skip the create-time scan until the reset has been cleared under the milestone write lock.
   Rationale: This breaks the bead's stated goal of keeping create/start semantics aligned. After a plan rematerialization, `run start` will clear stale lineage first, but `project create-from-bead` can now reject the same bead based on obsolete rows from the old plan.
2. finding_summary: Legacy active lineage rows without `run_id` are silently filtered out of `active_task_runs_for_bead`, which lets `project create-from-bead` create a second project even when the only existing row is an orphaned running entry.
affected_files: ["src/adapters/fs.rs", "src/cli/project.rs", "tests/cli.rs"]
file_refs: ["src/adapters/fs.rs:5089-5104", "src/adapters/fs.rs:5147-5154", "src/cli/project.rs:588-596", "tests/cli.rs:13549-13594"]
remediation_hint: Treat nonterminal runless rows as inconsistent active lineage for create-time validation and return the same repair-style error used for other orphaned states instead of dropping them from the scan.
   Rationale: The new helper intentionally hides exactly the upgraded/orphaned state that this bead was supposed to surface. The added CLI test locks in that false-negative path by asserting that a missing `legacy-project` row with `outcome:"running"` should allow a new project.
